intitule: Reading Comprehension: Conflict Resolution in the School Community

enonce.texte:
Read the meeting report below and answer the questions that follow.

SCHOOL PREFECTS' MEETING REPORT

Date: March 15, 2025

The school prefects held a meeting to discuss conflicts that have occurred among students. The meeting was chaired by the Head Prefect, who reminded everyone that conflicts can be resolved peacefully. The prefects discussed cases of bullying, which have been reported in the junior classes. They also talked about misunderstandings between students, that often arise during group work. The prefects, whose role is to maintain peace, proposed several solutions. They suggested peer mediation, which has worked well in other schools. They also recommended that students who have conflicts should talk to their class teachers. The prefects decided to organize a workshop on conflict resolution, that will take place next month. They hope that this workshop, which will be open to all students, will help create a more peaceful school environment.
images: An illustration of a school meeting room with students sitting around a table, with one student standing and speaking, holding a notebook.

questions.texte: Identify the date when the school prefects' meeting was held.
questions.reponse: The meeting was held on March 15, 2025.

questions.texte: Explain what types of conflicts were discussed during the meeting.
questions.reponse: The prefects discussed cases of bullying in the junior classes and misunderstandings between students during group work.

questions.texte: Justify why the prefects decided to organize a workshop on conflict resolution.
questions.reponse: The prefects decided to organize a workshop on conflict resolution to help create a more peaceful school environment. They hope that the workshop will teach students how to resolve conflicts peacefully.

questions.texte: Analyse the role of the school prefects in conflict resolution based on the text.
questions.reponse: The school prefects play a crucial role in conflict resolution. They identify problems, discuss solutions, and take action to maintain peace. They proposed peer mediation and encouraged students to talk to their teachers. By organizing a workshop, they are proactively addressing the issue and educating the school community on peaceful conflict resolution.

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct relative pronouns: who, which, that, whom, whose.

questions.texte: The student __________ resolved the conflict received a reward.
questions.reponse: The student who resolved the conflict received a reward.

questions.texte: The workshop, __________ was organized by the prefects, was very successful.
questions.reponse: The workshop, which was organized by the prefects, was very successful.

questions.texte: The teacher __________ advice I followed helped me solve the problem.
questions.reponse: The teacher whose advice I followed helped me solve the problem.

questions.texte: The students __________ we met at the meeting were very friendly.
questions.reponse: The students whom we met at the meeting were very friendly.

questions.texte: The conflict __________ happened in the classroom was resolved quickly.
questions.reponse: The conflict that happened in the classroom was resolved quickly.

questions.texte: The prefects, __________ role is to maintain peace, proposed several solutions.
questions.reponse: The prefects, whose role is to maintain peace, proposed several solutions.

intitule: Writing: Process Paragraph on Conflict Resolution

enonce.texte:
Write a process paragraph (about 100-120 words) explaining the steps to resolve a conflict in the school community.

Your paragraph should include:

  • A clear introduction stating the goal.
  • At least four steps in the process.
  • A conclusion that emphasizes the importance of the process.

Use at least three relative pronouns in your paragraph.

questions.texte: Write your process paragraph on conflict resolution in the school community.
questions.reponse: Resolving conflicts in the school community requires a clear process that everyone can follow. First, the students who are involved in the conflict should sit down and talk calmly about the problem. Next, they should identify the issue that caused the disagreement. After that, they can discuss possible solutions, which should be fair to both parties. Then, they should agree on a solution that satisfies everyone. Finally, they should follow up to ensure that the solution works. This process, which emphasizes communication and respect, helps create a peaceful school environment where everyone feels valued.

intitule: Writing: Narrative Paragraph on Conflict Resolution

enonce.texte:
Write a narrative paragraph (about 80-100 words) about a time when you witnessed or participated in resolving a conflict in your school.

Your paragraph should include:

  • A clear introduction of the situation.
  • A description of how the conflict was resolved.
  • A conclusion about the outcome.

questions.texte: Write your narrative paragraph about resolving a conflict in your school.
questions.reponse: Last semester, two of my classmates argued about a group project. They both wanted to be the leader and refused to work together. The teacher, who noticed the conflict, decided to mediate. She listened to both students and helped them understand each other's perspectives. Eventually, they agreed to share the leadership role, which made everyone happy. This experience taught me that communication is the key to resolving conflicts.

intitule: Grammar: Combining Sentences with Relative Pronouns

enonce.texte: Combine the following pairs of sentences using relative pronouns.

questions.texte: The student solved the conflict. The student was praised by the teacher.
questions.reponse: The student who solved the conflict was praised by the teacher.

questions.texte: The workshop was very helpful. The prefects organized the workshop.
questions.reponse: The workshop which the prefects organized was very helpful.

questions.texte: The teacher gave good advice. I followed the teacher's advice.
questions.reponse: The teacher whose advice I followed gave good advice.

questions.texte: The students spoke to the principal. The students' parents were also present.
questions.reponse: The students, whose parents were also present, spoke to the principal.

questions.texte: The conflict happened in the cafeteria. The conflict was resolved by the prefects.
questions.reponse: The conflict that happened in the cafeteria was resolved by the prefects.

enonce.texte:
You are a student journalist for your school newspaper. Write a newspaper brief (about 100-120 words) about a conflict resolution initiative that has taken place in your school.

Your brief should include:

  • A catchy headline.
  • Information about what happened.
  • The outcome of the initiative.

Use at least four relative pronouns in your article.

questions.texte: Write your newspaper brief about a conflict resolution initiative in your school.
questions.reponse:
PEACE BUILDERS: SCHOOL LAUNCHES CONFLICT RESOLUTION CLUB

Our school has launched a Conflict Resolution Club, which aims to help students resolve disputes peacefully. The club, which was initiated by the prefects, meets every Friday. Students who have conflicts are encouraged to visit the club. The counselors, whose expertise in mediation is well-known, lead the sessions. The club also organizes workshops that teach communication skills. Students who attend the workshops have reported positive changes in their relationships. The principal, who supports the initiative, believes that this club will create a more harmonious school environment. This initiative, which is student-led, has already resolved several conflicts this term.
